sn.s: Es nuestro aniversario de un año juntos.
sn.e: It's our anniversary of one year together.

sn.s: Quiero decirle a Lucía lo que siento de verdad.
sn.e: I want to tell Lucía what I truly feel.
"Lucía, I want you to know something important."

sn.s: "Desde que te conocí, mi vida cambió."
sn.e: "Since I met you, my life changed."
"I want you to understand how much I love you."

sn.s: Esta vez digo 'te amo', no solo 'te quiero'.
sn.e: This time I say 'te amo,' not just 'te quiero.'
"I feel lucky to have you," I add.

sn.s: A Lucía se le llenan los ojos de lágrimas.
"Yo también te amo, con toda mi alma."
sn.e: "I love you too, with all my soul."
"I hope we spend many more years together."

sn.s: "Te prometo cuidar de lo nuestro," le digo.
sn.e: "I promise to take care of what we have," I tell her.

sn.s: Decir 'te amo' en español ya no me da miedo.
sn.e: Saying 'I love you' in Spanish no longer scares me.

sn.s: El subjuntivo: quiero que sepás, ojalá pasemos.
sn.e: The subjunctive: I want you to know, I hope we spend.

sn.s: Es el idioma del corazón que espera y desea.
sn.e: It's the language of the heart that hopes and wishes.
